title: Senior Manager APAC, Environmental, Health and Safety (EH&S) International, Corporate Engineering & Facilities (CEF)
description:
An exciting opportunity has arisen within EH&S International, CEF team for a EH&S Senior Manager AsiaPac, based at our Singapore office. The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring EH&S compliance for the region. They will work closely with the Site Operations, HR & IT teams.

Job Overview

  • EH&S Programmes, Projects and General Support
  • Provide advice and support to projects to ensure EH&S considerations throughout the project lifecycle. (e.g. review of appropriate documentation)
  • Ensure EH&S requirement considerations for new sites – mobilisation checklist
  • Incident reporting tool & other EH&S tools – promote and manage the use of the tools by staff to include administrative requirements, training for sites and administrative tasks involved in rolling out updated tool to AsiaPac region
  • Ensures incidents are reported appropriately and investigations are completed correctly
  • Manage and lead the audit programme – audit prep, audits attendance, leading the completion of post audit actions
  • Manage the Driver Safety Programme – assist with the roll out of the driver safety programme and the ongoing management of the programme
  • Manage Ergonomics Program (advice, awareness, data gathering, actions), including programme implementation, where required
  • Manage purchase orders, liaising with suppliers, resolving EH&S payment issues, general administration, in line with budget
  • Conduct and/or support risk assessments and other consultancy services to control workplace hazards
  • Act as H&S Consultant for some sites
  • Support and manage interactions between sites in AsiaPac with consultants, where applicable, and arrange gap assessments, risk assessments, training, programme development, standard operating procedure (SOP) development, ergonomic assessments & general coordination of site visits, actions resolution.
  • Carry out risk assessments (general, back to work, pregnancy, ergonomic), as required
  • Lead EH&S drop & dial in sessions to minimise workplace EH&S issues
  • Policies, procedures & reporting to ensure compliance
  • Coordinate with guidance of Director, EH&S International, Corporate Engineering & Facilities (CEF), in ensuring performance, compliance and improving culture across all sites
  • Provide advice/assistance to all sites on health and safety requirements including audit preparation, follow up from audits and policies/SOPs. Track actions and provide updates
  • Coordinate with guidance of Director, EH&S International CEF with preparing and updating of EH&S Polices/Programmes/SOPs and distribution of these, as required
  • Complete site Emergency Management plans, as required.
  • Monitor records of EH&S against legal requirements and Document Retention Policy
  • Gather metrics data and ensuring timely submission by sites on EH&S
  • Gather data and coordinate the compiling of electronic reports for incidents, accidents, ill health data
  • Compile and the distribution of General Manager (GM) reports on health and safety for different Countries (general, periodic performance reports)
  • Assist with corporate requirements in environmental sustainability
  • Initiatives
  • Lead EH&S initiatives and campaigns for AsiaPac and aligning with other International offices and head office
  • Support EH&S global corporate initiatives and requirements including, leading of EH&S management systems programme for relevant EH&S International sites
  • Manage campaigns that seeks to continuously support improvement of EH&S performance
  • Communications
  • Manage EH&S content for webpages for all AsiaPac offices. Develop EH&S webpages for new sites
  • Coordination of EH&S content for Site Facilities Handbook
  • Newsletter – producing alerts and new versions regularly

Person Specification, Knowledge & Experience

  • Ability to influence and collaborate with senior leaders
  • NEBOSH Diploma or equivalent minimum
  • Educated to degree level or equivalent, is required
  • 10 or more years work experience in an EH&S role, with a significant part of this within the private sector and in an office environment
  • Willingness to travel (essential)
  • Excellent communication & interpersonal skills, are required
  • Positive, can-do attitude is necessary, and candidate must be a team player

postedCompany.description: Gilead Sciences, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company that has pursued and achieved breakthroughs in medicine for more than three decades, with the goal of creating a healthier world for all people. The company is committed to advancing innovative medicines to prevent and treat life-threatening diseases, including HIV, viral hepatitis and cancer. Gilead operates in more than 35 countries worldwide, with headquarters in Foster City, California.
postedCompany.name: GILEAD SCIENCES SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
